title: Earnings call: Pinterest reports robust growth in Q3 2024, eyes future expansion
text: Pinterest reported Q3 2024 earnings showing robust growth with revenue reaching $898 million (18% YoY growth). The company achieved net income of $30.6 million, up 354% year-over-year, representing a turning point for profitability. Global monthly active users increased 11% to 537 million. CFO Julia Donnelly attributed rising operating expenses to investments in R&D and hiring for employees with expertise in artificial intelligence. The company authorized a $2 billion share buyback program. For Q4 2024 guidance, Pinterest projected revenue between $1.125-1.145 billion, with the midpoint trailing analyst estimates of $1.143 billion, which disappointed some investors. Despite beating on top and bottom lines in Q3, shares declined 15% following the announcement due to the soft Q4 guidance. The company emphasized its commitment to long-term growth through AI investments and lower-funnel advertising improvements. Management highlighted the growing importance of Performance+ for advertiser results and the impact of various product innovations on user engagement and advertiser efficiency.
